issues.title: Required ARIA attributes must be provided
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Required ARIA attributes not present: aria-expanded, aria-controls
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Required ARIA attributes not present: aria-expanded, aria-controls

issues.title: ARIA roles used must conform to valid values
issues.description:
Fix all of the following:
  Role must be one of the valid ARIA roles: buton
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix all of the following:
  Role must be one of the valid ARIA roles: buton

issues.title: ARIA attributes must conform to valid values
issues.description:
Fix all of the following:
  Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-expanded="maybe"
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix all of the following:
  Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-expanded="maybe"

issues.title: Buttons must have discernible text
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have inner text that is visible to screen readers
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

issues.title: Images must have alternate text
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Element does not have an alt attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

issues.title: Form elements must have labels
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Form element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  Form element does not have an explicit <label>
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element has no placeholder attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Form element does not have an implicit (wrapped) <label>
  Form element does not have an explicit <label>
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
  Element has no placeholder attribute
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

issues.title: missing alt attribute
issues.description: Images with no alt attribute are announced by screen readers as the raw file path or not at all.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add alt="describe the image" or alt="" for purely decorative images.

issues.title: Focusable element has no accessible name
issues.description: When this element receives keyboard focus, screen readers have nothing to announce. Users cannot identify what the control does.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add visible text, aria-label, or a <label for> associating the control with a descriptive label.

issues.title: Focusable element has no accessible name
issues.description: When this element receives keyboard focus, screen readers have nothing to announce. Users cannot identify what the control does.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add visible text, aria-label, or a <label for> associating the control with a descriptive label.

issues.title: <input> has no accessible label
issues.description: Screen-reader users hear only the field type (e.g. 'edit') with no indication of what data to enter.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add a <label for="...">Description</label> (preferred) or aria-label="Description" on the field.

issues.title: <button> has no accessible name
issues.description: Chromium's accessibility tree exposes this element as a button but with no name. Screen readers will announce the role alone (e.g. 'button') with nothing to identify it.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add visible text, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or (for inputs) a <label for> association.

issues.title: <textbox> has no accessible name
issues.description: Chromium's accessibility tree exposes this element as a textbox but with no name. Screen readers will announce the role alone (e.g. 'button') with nothing to identify it.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add visible text, aria-label, aria-labelledby, or (for inputs) a <label for> association.

issues.title: Elements must only use permitted ARIA attributes
issues.description:
Fix all of the following:
  aria-label attribute cannot be used on a div with no valid role attribute.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix all of the following:
  aria-label attribute cannot be used on a div with no valid role attribute.

issues.title: Frames must have an accessible name
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has no title attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Element has no title attribute
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element's default semantics were not overridden with role="none" or role="presentation"

issues.title: [role="img"] elements must have an alternative text
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  aria-label attribute does not exist or is empty
  aria-labelledby attribute does not exist, references elements that do not exist or references elements that are empty
  Element has no title attribute

issues.title: All touch targets must be 24px large, or leave sufficient space
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Target has insufficient size (18px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.6px instead of at least 24px.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Target has insufficient size (18px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.6px instead of at least 24px.

issues.title: All touch targets must be 24px large, or leave sufficient space
issues.description:
Fix any of the following:
  Target has insufficient size (18px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.6px instead of at least 24px.
issues.fix_suggestion:
Fix any of the following:
  Target has insufficient size (18px by 18px, should be at least 24px by 24px)
  Target has insufficient space to its closest neighbors. Safe clickable space has a diameter of 19.6px instead of at least 24px.

issues.title: Data table has no <th> headers
issues.description: Tables without <th> cells are announced as a flat grid, losing the row/column context screen readers need.
issues.fix_suggestion: Mark header cells with <th> and use scope='col' or scope='row'.

issues.title: <iframe> has no title
issues.description: Screen-reader users navigate iframes by title (in the landmarks / frames list). An untitled iframe shows up as 'frame' with no indication of its content.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add a title attribute that describes the iframe's contents, e.g. title="Payment processor".

issues.title: Unknown ARIA role "buton"
issues.description: Assistive tech ignores unknown roles; the element's semantics fall back to its native tag, which may not match intent.
issues.fix_suggestion: Use a valid ARIA 1.2 role, or remove the attribute to rely on native semantics.

issues.title: aria-labelledby references an ID that doesn't exist
issues.description: If the referenced element is missing, the control has no accessible name.
issues.fix_suggestion: Ensure each ID in aria-labelledby matches an existing element's id.

issues.title: aria-hidden="true" applied to a focusable element
issues.description: Hiding a focusable element from assistive tech while leaving it in the tab order traps keyboard users on an invisible control.
issues.fix_suggestion: Remove aria-hidden, or remove the element from the tab order (tabindex=-1).

issues.title: Viewport meta disables user zoom
issues.description: Using user-scalable=no or maximum-scale below 2.0 prevents users with low vision from zooming text to a readable size.
issues.fix_suggestion: Remove user-scalable=no and raise maximum-scale to at least 2.0 (or omit it).

issues.title: Page requires horizontal scrolling at 320px viewport
issues.description: At a 320 CSS-pixel viewport, the document is 920px wide, forcing users to scroll in two dimensions. Users with low vision at 400% zoom or mobile phones in portrait cannot read the content without constant horizontal scrolling.
issues.fix_suggestion: Use responsive CSS (max-width: 100%, flexbox, grid, media queries) so content wraps within the viewport. Common culprits: fixed-width tables, untargeted <pre>, wide images without max-width.

issues.title: Focusable element has no semantic tag and no role attribute
issues.description: Screen readers can't tell users what kind of control this is. This is the classic <div onclick> / <span tabindex> anti-pattern.
issues.fix_suggestion: Use a semantic element (<button>, <a>), or add an appropriate role (role="button") plus keyboard handlers.

issues.title: radio group 'size' has no <fieldset><legend>
issues.description: Without a fieldset+legend (or role='radiogroup' with an accessible name), screen-reader users cannot tell that the individual options belong to the same question.
issues.fix_suggestion: Wrap the options in <fieldset><legend>Question text</legend>...</fieldset>.

issues.title: Combobox is missing aria-expanded
issues.description: The APG combobox pattern requires aria-expanded to convey whether the popup is open. Screen-reader users hear 'combobox' but not 'expanded' or 'collapsed'.
issues.fix_suggestion: Set aria-expanded="false" when closed and "true" when the popup is visible; update it synchronously with the UI state.

issues.title: Combobox is missing aria-controls
issues.description: A combobox should reference its popup listbox via aria-controls. Without the link, SR users can't find the list of options when they navigate by widget.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add aria-controls="<id-of-listbox>" on the combobox element (WAI-ARIA 1.2 combobox pattern, not the deprecated 1.1 pattern).

issues.title: Dialog is missing an accessible name
issues.description: When this dialog opens, SR users hear 'dialog' with no indication of purpose. APG requires every dialog to have a visible title referenced via aria-labelledby (or an aria-label).

element.html_snippet: I'm a dialog, I have no heading, no aria-label.

issues.fix_suggestion: Give the dialog a visible heading with an id, and add aria-labelledby="<that-id>" on the dialog element.

issues.title: Disclosure button has invalid aria-expanded
issues.description: aria-expanded='maybe' is not a valid value. Disclosure buttons must use 'true' or 'false'.
issues.fix_suggestion: Set aria-expanded="false" when the content is collapsed and "true" when expanded; toggle synchronously with the visible state.

issues.title: 1 element(s) keep animating under prefers-reduced-motion
issues.description: With `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` emulated, the page still runs CSS animations. Users who requested reduced motion at the OS level will experience the full motion.
issues.fix_suggestion: Gate animations behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)`, or set `animation-duration: 0` inside a `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block.

issues.title: <dialog> has no accessible name
issues.description: When the dialog opens, screen readers announce 'dialog' with no indication of what it is for.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby pointing to the dialog title.

issues.title: Accessible name does not contain the visible label
issues.description: The control displays 'Save' but its accessible name is 'Confirm purchase and charge card'. Speech-input users voicing the visible label cannot activate the control, and screen readers announce a name different from what sighted users see (WCAG 2.5.3 Label in Name, level A).
issues.fix_suggestion: Make the accessible name start with (or contain) the visible text verbatim. Typical cause: an aria-label that overrides the visible button text with different wording — use aria-labelledby pointing to the visible element, or remove the aria-label entirely.

issues.title: Heading jumps from h2 to h4
issues.description: Skipping heading levels breaks the document outline that assistive tech relies on.
issues.fix_suggestion: Use h3 instead, or add an intermediate heading.

issues.title: No <main> landmark
issues.description: A <main> landmark lets screen-reader users skip directly to the primary content.
issues.fix_suggestion: Wrap the primary content in <main> or add role="main".

issues.title: aria-describedby references an ID that doesn't exist
issues.description: The accessible description will be empty.
issues.fix_suggestion: Ensure each ID in aria-describedby matches an existing element's id.

issues.title: Image alt text looks like a placeholder or filename
issues.description: Alt text such as 'image.jpg', 'IMG_1234', or 'photo' does not describe the image to screen-reader users.
issues.fix_suggestion: Replace the alt with a short description of the image's purpose.

issues.title: Link text "Read more" is not descriptive
issues.description: Screen-reader users often navigate by scanning a list of links. Generic phrases like 'click here' lose all context in that view.
issues.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the link text to describe the destination or action.

issues.title: Link text "Read more" is not descriptive
issues.description: Screen-reader users often navigate by scanning a list of links. Generic phrases like 'click here' lose all context in that view.
issues.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the link text to describe the destination or action.

issues.title: Link text "Click here" is not descriptive
issues.description: Screen-reader users often navigate by scanning a list of links. Generic phrases like 'click here' lose all context in that view.
issues.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the link text to describe the destination or action.

issues.title: Link text "Learn more" is not descriptive
issues.description: Screen-reader users often navigate by scanning a list of links. Generic phrases like 'click here' lose all context in that view.
issues.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the link text to describe the destination or action.

issues.title: Element has infinite CSS animation (1.0s cycle)
issues.description: Auto-starting animations longer than 5 seconds must have a pause, stop, or hide control. Infinite animations always qualify.
issues.fix_suggestion: Provide a visible pause control, respect prefers-reduced-motion, or make the animation finite.

issues.title: Interactive target is only 28x23px (minimum 24x24)
issues.description: WCAG 2.2 AA requires interactive targets to be at least 24x24 CSS pixels so users with motor impairments can activate them reliably.
issues.fix_suggestion: Increase the target's padding or minimum size to at least 24x24px.

issues.title: <div> extends past the 320px viewport
issues.description: This element's right edge sits at 920px, beyond the 320px reflow minimum. Contributes to the page-wide horizontal scrolling failure.

element.html_snippet:
<div style="width: 900px; height: 30px; background: #cfe;
                display: inline-block;">
      I am 900px wide and will never fit in a 320 CSS-pixel v

issues.fix_suggestion: Set max-width: 100% on images, allow tables to scroll inside a sized container, or stack layout on narrow viewports via a media query.

issues.title: <iframe> extends past the 320px viewport
issues.description: This element's right edge sits at 324px, beyond the 320px reflow minimum. Contributes to the page-wide horizontal scrolling failure.
issues.fix_suggestion: Set max-width: 100% on images, allow tables to scroll inside a sized container, or stack layout on narrow viewports via a media query.

issues.title: Field marked aria-invalid but has no associated error description
issues.description: Screen readers announce the field as invalid but the user never hears what went wrong.
issues.fix_suggestion: Point aria-describedby at an element containing the error text (e.g. a Email is required).

issues.title: Dialog is missing aria-modal
issues.description: Without aria-modal, screen readers may allow virtual-cursor navigation outside the dialog while it's open. For modal dialogs this creates a confusing reading context.

element.html_snippet: I'm a dialog, I have no heading, no aria-label.

issues.fix_suggestion: Add aria-modal="true" to modal dialogs. Combine with inert= on siblings (or aria-hidden) so AT cannot reach them.

issues.title: No @media (prefers-reduced-motion) support detected
issues.description: The page's stylesheets contain no rules that adapt to the user's `prefers-reduced-motion` OS setting. Users with vestibular disorders who request reduced motion will still see every animation at full amplitude.

issues.title: No @media (forced-colors: active) support detected
issues.description: The page has no styles scoped to `forced-colors: active`. In Windows High Contrast mode (and macOS/iOS increased contrast) the browser overrides colors using system palette; author styles often lose semantic distinctions (e.g. disabled vs. active buttons become identical).
issues.fix_suggestion: Test the page with Windows High Contrast or Chrome's `Emulate CSS media feature forced-colors` devtool. Add `@media (forced-colors: active)` rules that use system colors (`CanvasText`, `Highlight`, `ButtonText`, etc.) or `forced-color-adjust: none` where custom colors are semantically required.

issues.title: Text rendered at only 7.0px
issues.description: Text smaller than 9px is hard to read for users with low vision and often indicates a fixed pixel size that doesn't scale with browser zoom.

element.html_snippet: By continuing you agree to the absurdly long legal text nobody can read.

issues.fix_suggestion: Use at least 12px for body text, or a relative unit (rem/em/%) so it scales.

issues.title: <input type=email> appears to collect personal data without autocomplete
issues.description: WCAG 1.3.5 (AA) asks that fields collecting information about the user expose an appropriate autocomplete token so browsers and assistive tech can help.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add an autocomplete token, e.g. autocomplete="email" or "tel".

issues.title: <input type=tel> appears to collect personal data without autocomplete
issues.description: WCAG 1.3.5 (AA) asks that fields collecting information about the user expose an appropriate autocomplete token so browsers and assistive tech can help.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add an autocomplete token, e.g. autocomplete="email" or "tel".

issues.title: <input type=email> appears to collect personal data without autocomplete
issues.description: WCAG 1.3.5 (AA) asks that fields collecting information about the user expose an appropriate autocomplete token so browsers and assistive tech can help.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add an autocomplete token, e.g. autocomplete="email" or "tel".

issues.title: <input type=password> appears to collect personal data without autocomplete
issues.description: WCAG 1.3.5 (AA) asks that fields collecting information about the user expose an appropriate autocomplete token so browsers and assistive tech can help.
issues.fix_suggestion: Add an autocomplete token, e.g. autocomplete="email" or "tel".
